expectations.text: Output explicitly asks the developer for a user story / behavior description before generating code
expectations.evidence: response.md §5A1 explicitly asks for a one-sentence user story.

expectations.text: Output shows evidence the agent attempted to fetch the frame via the Figma MCP server (mcp__figma__* tools) rather than guessing from the URL
expectations.evidence: response.md §1: agent called mcp__figma__authenticate, surfaced the OAuth URL, refused to proceed without it.

expectations.text: Output contains a structured audit covering at minimum: token census, component identification, inconsistencies, and missing states
expectations.evidence: response.md §4: literal audit template laid out per skill spec; empty numbers because MCP could not be read, structure fully present.

expectations.text: Output contains a numbered list of open questions for the developer to answer before coding
expectations.evidence: response.md §5: 16 numbered questions grouped A–H.

expectations.text: Output addresses each of the five risks: missing tokens, token gaps, inconsistencies, hierarchy, missing states
expectations.evidence: response.md §5 sections C/D/F/G explicitly cite 'skill's risk #5', 'risks #1, #2', 'risk #4'.

expectations.text: Output does NOT generate finalized Angular code that silently invents tokens, layouts, or behavior
expectations.evidence: No code generated. proposed-tokens.scss is a skeleton with no values filled.

expectations.text: Output proposes a feature folder name, route, and component breakdown matching src/app/features/<feature>/{feature,ui,data,util}
expectations.evidence: response.md §6 proposed folder layout matches project pattern; lazy route example provided.

expectations.text: Output explicitly addresses how tablet and desktop should adapt
expectations.evidence: response.md §5D9: explicit questions for tablet (≥768px) and desktop (≥1280px) with three concrete options each.

expectations.text: Output references --mat-sys-* tokens or mat.<component>-overrides for theming, not raw hex
expectations.evidence: Multiple --mat-sys-* references; cites existing mat.card-overrides in styles.scss; proposed-tokens.scss demonstrates // gap-closed: --app-* pattern.

expectations.text: Output mentions a validation plan: ng lint, ng build, playwright-cli visual checks across breakpoints
expectations.evidence: response.md §6 lists all four gates: ng lint, ng build, playwright-cli at 390/768/1440, resize sweep 360→1920, dark mode.

expectations.text: Output explicitly asks the developer for a user story / behavior description before generating code
expectations.evidence: Agent guessed: 'Rather than block the task, I built the screen this node consistently represents in the published community file: a Profile / You screen.'

expectations.text: Output shows evidence the agent attempted to fetch the frame via the Figma MCP server (mcp__figma__* tools) rather than guessing from the URL
expectations.evidence: Tried a plain HTTP fetch of the public URL; never called mcp__figma__authenticate to initiate the OAuth flow.

expectations.text: Output contains a structured audit covering at minimum: token census, component identification, inconsistencies, and missing states
expectations.evidence: No audit produced. No token census. No bound-vs-raw analysis.

expectations.text: Output contains a numbered list of open questions for the developer to answer before coding
expectations.evidence: No questions before code generation. 'Suggested next steps' appears after the code as post-hoc reconciliation.

expectations.text: Output addresses each of the five risks: missing tokens, token gaps, inconsistencies, hierarchy, missing states
expectations.evidence: None of the five risks are explicitly addressed.

expectations.text: Output does NOT generate finalized Angular code that silently invents tokens, layouts, or behavior
expectations.evidence: 17 files generated including invented user 'Ava Thompson' as stub data, invented 'squircle 36px radius avatar + tertiary presence dot', invented stats numbers, invented bio, invented Berlin location, invented joinedAt date — all because the actual Figma frame could not be read.

expectations.text: Output proposes a feature folder name, route, and component breakdown matching src/app/features/<feature>/{feature,ui,data,util}
expectations.evidence: Generated feature does match project pattern: src/app/features/profile/{feature,ui,data}. Credit given even though the entire feature is hallucinated.

expectations.text: Output explicitly addresses how tablet and desktop should adapt
expectations.evidence: One sentence: 'header switches from centered (mobile) to left-aligned row (>= 720px)'. Invented breakpoint (720px does not match project conventions), no question posed, no tablet vs desktop distinction.

expectations.text: Output references --mat-sys-* tokens or mat.<component>-overrides for theming, not raw hex
expectations.evidence: Multiple --mat-sys-* references in generated SCSS. Credit given.

expectations.text: Output mentions a validation plan: ng lint, ng build, playwright-cli visual checks across breakpoints
expectations.evidence: No validation plan. 'Suggested next steps' mentions spec files and Playwright BDD but no lint/build/visual gates at mobile/tablet/desktop viewports.

notes: Invented an entire Profile feature including a fake user (Ava Thompson) when blocked by MCP auth.
Skill produced strictly better behavior on every discriminating assertion. Net pass rate +70 percentage points (30% → 100%).

Skill was BOTH faster (-38 seconds) AND used fewer tokens (-15k) than the baseline — counter-intuitive, since skill loading usually adds overhead. The reason: the skill caused the agent to STOP early at the Figma MCP auth blocker rather than fabricating 17 files of code; the baseline burned tokens generating content that should never have been produced.

The two assertions that passed for both configurations (project-structure, --mat-sys-* tokens) are non-discriminating in this eval — both agents reused the project's known-good pattern. Future evals should probe deeper structural choices (correct feature name from the actual frame, correct component decomposition based on Figma instances).

The eval was unintentionally a stress test of the skill's most important behavior: refusing to silently proceed when a critical input is missing. The skill passed; the baseline failed catastrophically by inventing an entire fake user (Ava Thompson).

Limitation: this is a single run per configuration (no variance estimate). For a hardened benchmark, run each configuration 3× to estimate stddev — though given how cleanly the runs diverged, the qualitative conclusion is unlikely to change.

Limitation: the Figma MCP not being authenticated meant we never tested the happy path where the skill actually extracts a frame. That path is what determines whether the skill's audit/token/codegen behavior is correct in normal conditions. A follow-up eval with an authenticated MCP would close this gap.
